title: Visual notification of events
abstract: An apparatus (100) comprises a primary user interface, a housing (101) of non-translucent material, a slot (102, 103, 104) of translucent material arranged in the housing (101), the slot (102, 103, 104) having a primary function not related to user interaction, and a light source (106, 107, 108) arranged inside the housing (101) in proximity of the slot (102, 103, 104); wherein the apparatus (100) has a primary mode in which the primary user interface is on and a secondary mode in which the primary user interface is off, and wherein the apparatus (100) further comprises a controller (420) configured to provide visual notifications to a user with the light source (106, 107, 108) in response to predetermined events when the apparatus (100) is in the secondary mode.
claims: Claims 1. A device characterized in comprising: at least one display screen; a housing comprising non-translucent material; a slot in the housing, the slot having a primary function not related to user interaction; and at least one light source arranged inside the housing in proximity of the slot; wherein the device has a primary mode in which the at least one display screen is on and a secondary mode, in which the display screen is off; and wherein the device further comprises: a controller configured to provide visual notifications to a user with the at least one light source in response to predetermined events when the apparatus is in the secondary mode. 2. A method executed in a controller comprised in a device, characterized in comprising: detecting occurrence of an event; determining whether a device is in a primary mode or in a secondary mode, wherein in the primary mode, a primary user interface of the device is on and in the secondary mode a primary user interface of the device is off; providing a visual notification in response to the event, if the device is determined to be in the secondary mode; wherein providing a visual notification comprises: operating a light source arranged in proximity of a slot in a non-translucent housing of the device, wherein primary function of the slots is unrelated to visual notifications. 3. The method as claimed in claim 2, further comprising categorizing the detected events, and providing different visual notifications for events of different categories. 4. The method as claimed in claim 2 or 3, wherein visual notifications in response to events of different categories differ in one or more of: a pattern of turning on/off of the light source, a color sequence for the light source, a strobe frequency for the light source, and an intensity of light emitted by the light source. 5. The method as claimed in any of the claims 2-4, wherein the categorization of an event is based on one or more of: priority of the event, originator of the event and nature of the event. 6. An apparatus, characterized in comprising: a primary user interface; a housing comprising non-translucent material; a slot of translucent material arranged in the housing, the slot having a primary function not related to user interaction; and a light source arranged inside the housing in proximity of the slot; wherein the apparatus has a primary mode in which the primary user interface is on and a secondary mode in which the primary user interface is off; and wherein the apparatus further comprises: a controller configured to provide visual notifications to a user with the light source in response to predetermined events when the apparatus is in the secondary mode. 7. The apparatus as claimed in claim 6, wherein the housing comprises a conductive material and wherein the primary function of the slot is allowing operation of an antenna. 8. The apparatus as claimed in claim 6 or 7, wherein the predetermined event is arrival of one or more of: an electronic mail message, a text message, a multimedia message, an instant message, a voice call, a voice over internet protocol message, and a video call, or one or more of: expiry of a time period, a reminder, or an alarm, a state of a digital assistant. 9. The apparatus as claimed in any of claims 6-8, wherein the visual notification comprises one or more of: turning on and off of the light source, strobing of the light source, and changing the light emitted by the light source. 10. The apparatus as claimed in any of claims 6-9, wherein the light source comprises at least one Light Emitting Diode. 11. The apparatus as claimed in any of claims 6-10, wherein the controller is configured to categorize the detected predetermined events and provide different visual notifications for different categories of events. 12. The apparatus as claimed in claim 11, wherein the visual notifications for different categories differ in at least one of color of the visual notification, duration of the visual notification or frequency of on-off cycles of light source. 13. The apparatus as claimed in any of claims 6-12, wherein in the secondary mode a speaker of the apparatus is turned off or a haptic actuator of the apparatus is turned off or both a speaker and a haptic actuator of the apparatus are turned off. 14. The apparatus as claimed in any of claims 6-13, comprising multiple slots of translucent material, the slots having a primary function not related to user interaction and a light source arranged in proximity of each of the multiple slots. 15. The apparatus as claimed in claim 14, wherein light sources corresponding to different slots are configured to emit light of different colors.
